metadata.description: Maps each deduplicated threat to its discovering Phase B specialists for confidence analysis

TECH-H001.title: Genesis Depositors Steal Accumulated Gauge Rewards via Missing userRewardPerTokenPaid Checkpoint
TECH-H001.duplicate_group_description: Genesis depositors steal accumulated gauge rewards because depositsForGenesis calls updateReward(address(0)) instead of updateReward(_tokenOwner), leaving userRewardPerTokenPaid at 0

TECH-H002.title: RouterV2.swapExactTokensForTokensSimple Sends Tokens to address(0) Due to Uninitialized Route Fields
TECH-H002.duplicate_group_description: swapExactTokensForTokensSimple is completely non-functional because routes[0].pair and routes[0].receiver are never initialized, defaulting to address(0)

TECH-H003.title: Fee-on-Transfer Swap Router Uses Wrong Pool for Amount Calculation Causing Reverts or Suboptimal Swaps
TECH-H003.duplicate_group_description: _swapSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens uses getAmountOut which returns best of stable/volatile, but executes swap on the specific pool type in routes[i].stable, causing K-invariant failures

TECH-H004.title: TradeHelper._pairFor Computes Incorrect CREATE2 Addresses Breaking GlobalRouter Swaps
TECH-H004.duplicate_group_description: TradeHelper._pairFor uses wrong CREATE2 deployer (PairFactory instead of PairGenerator) and incomplete init code hash, making GlobalRouter completely broken

TECH-H005.title: RouterV2._swap Ignores _to Parameter Using routes[i].receiver Instead, Breaking swapExactTokensForETH
TECH-H005.duplicate_group_description: RouterV2._swap ignores its _to parameter entirely, using routes[i].receiver for all output routing, breaking swapExactTokensForETH and violating API contract

TECH-H006.title: TradeHelper.getAmountsIn Infinite Loop Due to uint Underflow in Decrementing Loop Counter
TECH-H006.duplicate_group_description: TradeHelper.getAmountsIn has an infinite loop / immediate revert due to unsigned integer underflow when decrementing loop counter past 0

TECH-H007.title: TradeHelper.getAmountInStable Uses Inverted Formula (Addition Instead of Subtraction) Producing Wrong Quotes
TECH-H007.duplicate_group_description: TradeHelper.getAmountInStable uses y_1 = reserveOut + output (should be minus) and wrong decimal conversion, returning completely incorrect values

OPS-H005.title: GlobalRouter.exactInput Is a No-Op That Silently Returns Zero and Traps ETH
OPS-H005.duplicate_group_description: GlobalRouter.exactInput function body is entirely commented out, silently returning 0 and potentially trapping ETH

TECH-H012.title: GaugeExtraRewarder Uses TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) Instead of _totalSupply Creating Reward Distribution Mismatch
TECH-H012.duplicate_group_description: GaugeExtraRewarder uses TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) instead of gauge._totalSupply for reward calculations, enabling dilution via direct LP token donations

TECH-H013.title: Unrestricted Pair.claimStakingFees() enables anyone to siphon staking-fee bucket to a potentially unset/incorrect handler (fund loss/DoS)
TECH-H013.duplicate_group_description: Pair.claimStakingFees is permissionless and sends fees to PairFactory.stakingFeeHandler which may be unset (address(0)), burning accumulated fees

ECON-H017.title: Pair TWAP Oracle calculates ratio of average reserves instead of average price
ECON-H017.duplicate_group_description: Pair TWAP oracle accumulates time-weighted reserves instead of time-weighted prices, making it mathematically incorrect and more manipulable

ECON-H018.title: RouterV2 removeLiquidityETHSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens sweeps entire token balance
ECON-H018.duplicate_group_description: removeLiquidityETHSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens sweeps router's entire token balance instead of just the amount from liquidity removal

ECON-H008.title: Pair Mint and Burn Not Paused During Swap Pause Enabling Stale-Price Liquidity Manipulation
ECON-H008.duplicate_group_description: Pair.mint and Pair.burn lack isPaused check while swap has it, enabling liquidity manipulation during emergency pause

ECON-H005.title: CustomPoolDeployer.createCustomPool Lacks Access Control Allowing Arbitrary Pool Creation with Manipulated Prices
ECON-H005.duplicate_group_description: CustomPoolDeployer.createCustomPool lacks access control (unlike other privileged functions that use onlyAuthorized), allowing arbitrary pool creation and registry pollution

ECON-H012.title: RouterV2 swapExactTokensForTokens Sends Initial Tokens to User-Controlled routes[0].pair Instead of Actual Pair
ECON-H012.duplicate_group_description: RouterV2.swapExactTokensForTokens sends tokens to user-controlled routes[0].pair but executes swap on computed pairFor() address, enabling extraction of excess pair balances

OPS-H006.title: Unbounded Referral Fee Setting Can DoS All Swaps via Arithmetic Underflow
OPS-H006.duplicate_group_description: PairFactory.setReferralFee has no upper bound validation; setting > 10000 causes arithmetic underflow in _update0/_update1, DoSing all swaps

OPS-H014.title: Permissionless GaugeFactory.createGauge enables attacker-controlled gauges that may be whitelisted downstream, enabling reward/fee capture or DoS
OPS-H014.duplicate_group_description: GaugeFactory.createGauge is permissionless, allowing arbitrary gauge deployment that can pollute registries and confuse UIs/admins

TECH2-H009.title: WAVAX withdraw uses 2300-gas `transfer`, potentially freezing unwrap for contracts and breaking router flows that unwrap to smart wallets
TECH2-H009.duplicate_group_description: Fee accumulator precision loss in Pair._update0/_update1 where amount * 1e18 / totalSupply rounds to 0 for small fees, permanently locking dust in PairFees

TECH2-H010.title: GaugeExtraRewarder pending reward calculation may underflow and revert due to rounding/precision mismatch, blocking gauge operations via callback
TECH2-H010.duplicate_group_description: GaugeExtraRewarder._pendingReward can underflow when computed accrued amount is less than rewardDebt due to rounding, blocking gauge operations via callback revert

OPS2-H008.title: WAVAX Withdrawals DoS for Smart Contracts via .transfer
OPS2-H008.duplicate_group_description: WAVAX.withdraw uses payable(msg.sender).transfer(wad) with 2300 gas stipend, preventing smart contracts with complex receive functions from withdrawing

ECON-H014.title: RouterV2 UNSAFE_swapExactTokensForTokens allows draining router token balances
ECON-H014.duplicate_group_description: UNSAFE_swapExactTokensForTokens accepts user-supplied amounts[] without validation, enabling extraction of excess pair balances or router balances through crafted swap parameters

ECON2-H016.title: GaugeV2 _withdraw Missing Explicit Balance Bound Check Creates Genesis Pool Desync Risk
ECON2-H016.duplicate_group_description: GaugeV2 withdraw/emergencyWithdraw paths lack explicit amount <= _balanceOf check, relying on _deductBalance which can desync _totalSupply if genesis pool behaves inconsistently

TECH-H008.title: PairFactory.setReferralFee Has No Upper Bound Allowing DoS of All Swaps via Arithmetic Revert

TECH-H009.title: GaugeV2.setInternalBribe Tautological Validation Allows Setting address(0) Breaking Fee Distribution

TECH-H010.title: GaugeV2.emergencyWithdrawAmount Lacks Balance Validation Enabling Potential Accounting Corruption

TECH-H011.title: GlobalRouter.exactInput Is a No-Op Function Body Silently Returning Zero Tokens

TECH-H014.title: Permissionless GlobalRouter.swapExactTokensForTokens ignores _type flag and may route through wrong pair if TradeHelper.pairFor is inconsistent with factory registry

TECH-H015.title: Pair.sample() and Pair.quote() lack bounds checks, enabling attacker-triggered reverts that can break any on-chain flow that depends on them (oracle/route DoS)

TECH-H016.title: RouterV2.removeLiquidityETHSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens transfers full router token balance, enabling cross-user donation theft (balance contamination)

TECH-H017.title: TradeHelper.getAmountsIn() infinite loop (uint underflow) can brick any on-chain integration relying on it for required-input computation

TECH-H018.title: BlackholePairAPIV2.getAmountOut() can be abused to force extremely high gas usage (quadratic scanning) in on-chain calls, potentially breaking integrators that call it in transactions

TECH-H019.title: BlackholePairAPIV2.initialize allows setting arbitrary external dependencies; misconfiguration could make API return misleading routing/pricing and break downstream automation

TECH-H020.title: Pair.permit() recomputes DOMAIN_SEPARATOR each call and stores it; potential signature confusion across forks or name changes (non-standard EIP-2612 behavior)

ECON-H001.title: Genesis Depositors Steal Accumulated Gauge Rewards via Missing Reward Checkpoint

ECON-H002.title: Fee-on-Transfer Swap Routing Selects Wrong Pool for Amount Calculation Causing Systematic Failures

ECON-H004.title: RouterV2._swap Ignores _to Parameter - swapExactTokensForETH Fails When Route Receiver Misconfigured

ECON-H006.title: TradeHelper.getAmountInStable Returns Incorrect Values Due to Wrong Formula Direction and Decimal Conversion

ECON-H007.title: GaugeExtraRewarder Uses Token Balance Instead of Gauge TotalSupply for Reward Distribution

ECON-H009.title: Pair.claimStakingFees Permissionless With Potentially Unset StakingFeeHandler Burns Accumulated Fees

ECON-H010.title: Unbounded Referral Fee Setting Allows Fee Manager to Divert 100% of Swap Fees or DoS All Swaps

ECON-H011.title: GaugeV2.setInternalBribe Tautological Validation Allows Zero Address Breaking Fee Distribution

ECON-H013.title: GaugeV2 genesis depositors can steal historical rewards

ECON-H015.title: RouterV2 swapExactTokensForTokens allows stealing excess pair balances

ECON-H016.title: GlobalRouter is completely broken due to incorrect Pair address computation

ECON-H020.title: Pair stable curve _k calculation rounds to zero for small balances

ECON-H021.title: RouterV2 multi-hop swaps mixing CL and basic pools leak value or revert

ECON-H022.title: TradeHelper getAmountInVolatile rounds down causing exact output swaps to revert

ECON-H023.title: PairFactory setReferralFee lacks upper bound allowing DoS of all swaps

ECON-H024.title: PairFactory setGenesisStatus allows bricking uninitialized pairs

OPS-H012.title: RouterV2 route.pair / route.receiver injection can drain Pair reserves using donated/stranded balances

OPS-H013.title: Pair oracle math uses TWAR (time-weighted average reserves) not TWAP, enabling manipulable on-chain pricing for dependent systems

OPS-H015.title: GlobalRouter.swapExactTokensForTokens uses TradeHelper.pairFor (likely wrong CREATE2 derivation) causing swaps to send funds to wrong addresses

OPS-H016.title: Public Pair.skim can be used to steal any tokens mistakenly sent to Pair contracts (non-reserve excess)

OPS-H017.title: PairFactory.setReferralFee and setCustomReferralFee can DoS all swaps if set > 10000 (underflow in Pair fee update)

OPS-H018.title: Genesis deposits can retroactively earn past Gauge rewards due to updateReward(address(0)) in depositsForGenesis

OPS-H019.title: GaugeV2.setInternalBribe zero-address check is broken, enabling misconfiguration that permanently bricks fee forwarding
